security_deposit.interest_rule: Required — must be held in interest-bearing account; interest paid annually to tenant
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: Varies — confirm required notice form (pay-or-quit, demand for possession, etc.).
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: Varies — confirm notice type and cure rules.
eviction.timeline_estimate_rule: 4-8 weeks uncontested; longer for contested cases
eviction.court_process_summary_rule: All evictions must be based on just cause under the Anti-Eviction Act (N.J.S.A. § 2A:18-61.1 et seq.). Court process is required. Just causes include: nonpayment, habitability violations by tenant, tenant at fault, or landlord good faith use (owner move-in, demolition, conversion). Landlord use evictions require relocation assistance in many municipalities.
eviction.filing_court_rule: All evictions must be based on just cause under the Anti-Eviction Act (N.J.S.A. § 2A:18-61.1 et seq.). Court process is required. Just causes include: nonpayment, habitability violations by tenant, tenant at fault, or landlord good faith use (owner move-in, demolition, conversion). Landlord use evictions require relocation assistance in many municipalities.
eviction.service_of_process_rule: All evictions must be based on just cause under the Anti-Eviction Act (N.J.S.A. § 2A:18-61.1 et seq.). Court process is required. Just causes include: nonpayment, habitability violations by tenant, tenant at fault, or landlord good faith use (owner move-in, demolition, conversion). Landlord use evictions require relocation assistance in many municipalities.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: Varies — commonly inspections, repairs, services, and showing the unit.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.cap_rule: No statewide rent control; local rules may apply.
rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: Varies — confirm your lease terms and statute.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: Varies — generally allowed with proper notice.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: Varies — confirm written notice and delivery method requirements.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: Varies — confirm landlord repair/habitability duties in statute and local code.
habitability.tenant_notice_required_rule: Varies — confirm required notice/cure steps before remedies.
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: Varies — some states allow termination after notice and failure to repair.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.

sources.title: Bootstrapped from app/data/state_laws.json

New Jersey caps security deposits at 1.5 months' rent. Landlords must return deposits within 30 days with an itemized statement of deductions. Deposits must be held in a federally insured interest-bearing account, with interest paid annually to the tenant. In cases involving fire, flood, condemnation, or evacuation, the deadline is reduced to 5 business days.
extractedQuotes: Maximum amount: 1.5 months' rent

sources.title: All evictions must be based on just cause under the Anti-Eviction Act (N.J.S.A. § 2A:18-61.1 et seq.). Court process is required. Just causes include: nonpayment, habitability violations by tenant, tenant at fault, or landlord good faith use (owner move-in, demolition, conversion). Landlord use evictions require relocation assistance in many municipalities.

Landlord use evictions require relocation assistance in many municipalities.

sources.title: No specific statutory notice period, but NJ courts recognize a tenant's right to privacy. Landlords may enter only for reasonable purposes (repairs, inspections shown to tenant, emergency) with reasonable advance notice. Emergency entry is permitted without prior notice.
extractedQuotes: No specific statutory notice period, but NJ courts recognize a tenant's right to privacy. Landlords may enter only for reasonable purposes (repairs, inspections shown to tenant, emergency) with reasonable advance notice. Emergency entry is permitted without prior notice.

sources.title: No statewide rent control. Over 100 municipalities have local rent control ordinances. Rent increase notice requirements vary by municipality. Rent increases on rent-controlled units are typically capped by local ordinance.

sources.title: New Jersey recognizes an implied warranty of habitability (Marini v. Ireland, 56 N.J. 130 (1970)) and codified landlord duties under N.J.S.A. § 2A:42-10.10. Landlords must keep premises fit and habitable. Tenants have remedies including rent escrow, repair and deduct (costs over $100 and under one month's rent, with 10-day notice), and rent withholding. Retaliatory eviction is prohibited.
extractedQuotes: New Jersey recognizes an implied warranty of habitability (Marini v. Ireland, 56 N.J. 130 (1970)) and codified landlord duties under N.J.S.A. § 2A:42-10.10. Landlords must keep premises fit and habitable. Tenants have remedies including rent escrow, repair and deduct (costs over $100 and under one month's rent, with 10-day notice), and rent withholding. Retaliatory eviction is prohibited.
